recommendation.summary: It's the first Sunday of July in Ann Arbor — a week stacked with culture (Celtic Festival, Rolling Sculpture, Sonic Lunch, James Hunter Six, Lucy Wainwright Roche) and two active job searches closing soon. With no activity log to balance against, the structural risk this week is coasting on passive cultural consumption while the Subsurface CFP deadline (July 17 — 12 days away) slips. Concrete next step: block 90 minutes this week to draft a Subsurface CFP abstract on AI governance or data strategy. Weather is mixed (storms likely Sun/Mon/Thu), making YMCA the go-to for health. Storm-threatened evenings are ideal for applications or CFP writing.

suggested.title: Subsurface 2026 CFP — deadline July 17 at 11:59 PM PT. Virtual data/AI open lakehouse conference. Submit a talk on AI governance frameworks, responsible AI, or data strategy. Draft abstract this week before Art Fair.
YMCA Downtown (400 W Washington St) — open 5:30 AM–9 PM weekdays, 7 AM–6 PM weekends. Especially recommended Mon (40% storms) and Thu (58% storms). Pool ideal on hot days (89°F Wed).
The James Hunter Six at The Ark (Jul 11, $40) — GRAMMY-nominated British soul. Pairs well with Michigan Celtic Festival the same day. Culture-dense Saturday.
